name: 55 · Crawl Campaign (cursor loop + batch-fold sink — the at-scale crawl shape)
description:
The PRODUCTION shape for big crawls (docs/32 batch-fold): a cursor-loop campaign whose crawl chunks publish their batches straight to the durable `INVENTORY_FOLD` stream, folded set-based into `file_inventory` by mekhan's fold consumer. Per-file rows NEVER become engine tokens — the net carries only a resume cursor and counts, so the same template that walks the 63 seeded files here walks a 4-million-file NAS without growing a single token.

  Start{file_server} → seed (write ~63 files into /tmp/mekhan-campaign-demo) → Loop `campaign` [ crawl chunk ] → End.

Each iteration runs the file-ops `crawl` op with `max_batches: 2` × `batch_size: 10` (so one chunk ≤ 20 files), `resume_from: "{{ campaign.cursor }}"` (a Loop-accumulator borrow interpolated into the config — empty on iteration 0), and `sink: {mode: "index", file_server_id: …}`. In sink mode the op publishes each filled batch durably (publish-ack BEFORE the cursor advances) and emits NO channel items; mekhan's `inventory.fold.batch.>` consumer upserts the rows idempotently on `(file_server_id, path)`, stamping `endpoint_root` + the runner's `serve_group` into provenance — so a later file-server `adopt` auto-fills a servable endpoint (zero-config downloads). The loop's accumulators thread the state: `cursor` folds `crawl.last_path`, `total` sums `crawl.count`; the loop exits when `crawl.exhausted` (lister EOF, not a max_batches stop) goes true. 63 files / 20 per chunk = 4 iterations (20+20+20+3).

Contrast with demo 50, which streams crawl batches as engine channel tokens into a `join: gather` barrier — great for SMALL crawls feeding an in-net consumer, but the gathered collection token grows with the corpus. This campaign shape is the one to copy for real file servers: chunked (cancel/resume at chunk granularity via the cursor), durable (JetStream publish-ack + idempotent upserts make retries harmless), and flat (engine work per iteration is constant).

Run it against your dev stack: `mekhan run demos/55-crawl-campaign --server http://localhost:13100 --input start.file_server=campaign-nas` (point `--server` at your slot's mekhan port). Self-contained: stdlib-only seed, local fs storage, no credentials.
